boards.name: Museum Board

meetings.summary:
# Museum Board Meeting Summary

**April 1, 2026**

## Key Decisions
- Approved minutes from the March 4, 2026 meeting
- Approved opening all workshop time slots to residents of all ages to improve access
- Approved proposed workshop schedule that allows staff setup, cleanup time, and visitor museum tours
- Decided to have students lead workshops with support from Museum Manager and Board members
- Approved plan to take basket-making workshop to the library in fall 2026, following summer feedback
- Scheduled rope crafts training session for Board volunteers before the next meeting

## Main Topics
- **Building repairs and inspection**: Foundation repairs are complete; staff will schedule an inspection before the 2026 season opening
- **Workshop planning**: The Board discussed timing, age accessibility, and logistics for upcoming butter-making, ice-cream-making, and rope-crafts workshops. Posters for butter and ice-cream workshops will be distributed by Board members
- **Heritage Day**: The Board reviewed 2025 attendance numbers and discussed fundraising ideas for the August 2026 event. They requested that Fire Halls display promotional signs closer to the event date
- **Exhibits and programs**: Work continues on "The Waterway Journey to Nipissing" exhibit and the Remembrance Interactive program. The Board received positive feedback on the Streams and Ladders game distributed during the 2025 Fire Department Christmas Run

## Notable Items
- Museum season opening is approaching; foundation inspection is pending
- Rope crafts training session will occur before the next Board meeting
- Workshop posters will be available at the May meeting for community distribution
- Heritage Day fundraising and event details require further discussion at the next meeting
- Artifact cataloguing and data entry will resume during the museum season

---
*Next regular meeting: Wednesday, May 6, 2026 at 6:00 p.m.*

# Museum Board Meeting Summary

**Mar 4, 2026**

## Key Decisions
- Jane Aultman appointed as Chairperson for 2026
- November 5, 2025 meeting minutes approved
- Board adjourned at 6:18 p.m.

## Main Topics
- **Building inspection**: An engineer will inspect the Museum before it opens for the season.
- **Event planning and volunteers**: Board members are being asked to volunteer for scheduled events. Summer students will help support events. The band "Country Re-Runs and Friends" was suggested as live entertainment for Heritage Day.
- **Exhibit development**: Staff have been developing "The Waterway Journey to Nipissing" as the theme for Heritage Day and a Remembrance Interactive exhibit program. The Board discussed applying for grants to create a traveling Remembrance Day exhibit that could visit the Powassan Library after being installed at the Museum.
- **Cataloguing**: Artifact cataloguing and data entry will resume during the Museum season.
- **Community feedback**: The Board is gathering feedback on a museum-designed game used during the 2025 Christmas event through Facebook and community discussion to guide future projects.

## Notable Items
- Engineer inspection of the Museum is scheduled before opening
- Heritage Day will feature "The Waterway Journey to Nipissing" theme
- Remembrance Interactive exhibit will be installed at the Museum this year to test it before potential grant-funded traveling exhibit
- Summer students will support seasonal events

---
*Next regular meeting: Wednesday, April 1, 2026 at 6:00 p.m.*

boards.description: Preservation and display of the history and heritage of Nipissing Township.

boards.name: Recreation Committee

meetings.summary:
# Recreation Committee Meeting Summary

**March 2, 2026**

## Key Decisions
- Approved February 2, 2026 meeting minutes
- Designated $195.00 in Chair Yoga donations to fund 2 additional yoga classes and support the Powassan & Callander Food Banks
- Extended the Winter Cards Program through the end of March 2026
- Approved adjustments to next year's Spaghetti Dinner: moving time to 4:00–7:00 p.m., increasing meatballs from 3 to 4 cases, and increasing garlic bread quantity

## Main Topics
- **Fundraising**: Bottle drive collected $364.80 total ($226.80 from Bearcreek Landfill and $138.00 in cash donations). Wolfe Lake Landfill collection is pending rescheduling.
- **Upcoming Events**: Easter Event (April 4th), Trivia Night (March 21st), Soccer Season (May 7–June 25th), and Mother's Day Craft (date to be determined, possibly cancelled).
- **Program Feedback**: Corn Hole and Cards programs are well-attended and popular. Corn Hole timing will shift to 7:00–9:00 p.m. with potential for outdoor expansion in warmer months.
- **Recent Event Reviews**: Family Skate drew 180 people and came in under budget ($1,623.22 of $1,700.00). Spaghetti Dinner served 97 people and raised $1,403.25.

## Notable Items
- Easter Event moved indoors to Community Centre to accommodate weather
- Soccer registration closes April 15, 2026; season runs May 7–June 25 for ages 4–12
- Chair Yoga final session: April 20, 2026
- Trivia Night: March 21, 2026 at 7:00 p.m. ($20 per team)
- Family Skate leftover marshmallows will be used at Easter Event

---
*Next regular meeting: May 4, 2026*

# Recreation Committee Meeting Summary
**Nipissing Township, Ontario – February 2, 2026**

I apologize, but the PDF file you've provided appears to be corrupted or improperly encoded. The content stream is compressed and unreadable, so I cannot access the actual meeting minutes to summarize the discussions, decisions, budgets, programs, or facilities that were addressed.

To help you, I would need either:
- A properly formatted and readable PDF file
- The meeting minutes in text or Word document format
- A scanned image of the document that can be read

If you can provide a readable version of the minutes, I'd be happy to create a 2-3 sentence plain-language summary for residents.

# Recreation Committee Meeting Summary

**Jan 19, 2026**

## Key Decisions
- Calista Bateman appointed as Chairperson for 2026
- November 17, 2025 meeting minutes approved
- Motion passed to distribute $425.45 in Chair Yoga donations: $150 to Powassan Food Bank, $150 to Callander Food Bank, and $125.45 to Recreation Account
- 2026 meeting dates deferred for staff review due to scheduling conflicts and community programming needs
- New winter yoga program discussion postponed to next meeting pending additional information

## Main Topics
- **Meeting scheduling**: Committee discussed potential conflicts with November 30 and the impact of evening meetings on community programs like yoga. Staff will review calendar options and return with alternatives.
- **Chair Yoga program**: Successfully resumed with 12 sessions scheduled. First 10 sessions funded by the committee; remaining two funded by donations. Average attendance is 23–24 participants. Defibrillator orientation was provided.
- **Bottle drive fundraiser**: Collected $363.40 from both landfill locations. Committee thanked volunteers including Councillors Stephen Kirkey and James Scott.
- **Spaghetti Dinner**: Upcoming event with ticket prices at $15 (adults) and $8 (children). Goal is to break even while building community engagement. Menu includes spaghetti, meatballs, garlic bread, and desserts. Volunteers confirmed and promotion planned via flyers, signage, and social media.
- **Family Skate event**: Confirmed fireworks, hot chocolate and marshmallow station, and participation by Powassan Voodoos. DJ confirmation pending. Outdoor ice conditions reported as excellent.

## Notable Items
- Chair Yoga donations totaling $425.45 distributed to local food banks and recreation account
- Bottle drive raised $363.40 from community volunteers
- Chair Yoga averaging 23–24 participants per session
- Spaghetti Dinner ticket prices: $15 adults, $8 children
- New winter yoga program details to be reviewed at next meeting

---
*Next regular meeting: March 2, 2026*
